title: Implement a Simple Book API
statement:
Create a RESTful API using Express.js to manage a collection of books. The API should have three endpoints: one to retrieve all books, one to create a new book, and one to retrieve a book by its ID.

The book collection should be stored in an in-memory array. When creating a new book, ensure the ID is automatically generated as a unique incrementing integer.

Your API should be able to handle JSON payloads and respond with JSON in all endpoints.

Implement the following endpoints:

### GET /api/books

Retrieves all books from the collection.

### POST /api/books

Creates a new book in the collection.

### GET /api/books/:id

Retrieves a book from the collection by its ID.

### Constraints

* Use only the in-memory array provided.
* Do not store any books in a database.
* Use a JSON schema to validate incoming request bodies.
* Handle errors properly (e.g., 404 Not Found, 400 Bad Request).
constraints: Use only the in-memory array provided.
Do not store any books in a database.

endpoints.description: Retrieves all books from the collection.

Creates a new book in the collection. Expected request body:

```json
{
  "title": "Book Title",
  "author": "Author Name"
}
```
Retrieves a book from the collection by its ID. Example: /api/books/1
